title: VariableEntry
description: One design-token variable. Used by Figma and Webflow contracts via $ref. Schema is Figma-first: the Webflow side uses the same shape with aliasOf pointing to the Figma source.

name.description: Display name. May change across renames. Do NOT use as sync key.

figmaId.description: Figma variable id. Stable across renames, machines, people. REQUIRED for cross-machine sync. Pattern: VariableID:<id>:<index>. The id part can be a hex string, a UUID, 'tpl-<hash>' for templates, or 'example-<slug>' for documentation.

webflowId.description: Webflow variable id once synced. Set by the write step, not the producer.

namespace.description: Webflow CSS variable namespace. The user's CSS uses --_theme---*, --_typography---*, --_sizes---*, etc. Pick the closest match; use 'other' if no good fit.

value.description: Primary mode value. Flattened from valuesByMode[default] in Figma. Type matches the 'type' field — string for colors, number for sizes, etc.

modes.description: mode_id → value. e.g. { 'default': '#000', 'dark': '#fff' }. Keys are normalized: 'default' for the primary mode, 'dark' for the dark theme, etc. Raw Figma keys like 'Mode 1:1' must be normalized before this point. At minimum, the 'default' key is required.

unit.description: CSS unit if applicable. Allowed: 'rem', 'px' (1px borders only), '%'. Process rejects em/vh/vw/pt per CLAUDE.md.

aliasOf.description: Figma variable id this aliases, if type='alias'. Required for alias chains like var(--_theme---text-color--primary) = var(--neutral--black).

mode.description: Legacy: singular mode name. V3.1+ uses `modes` (plural object). Kept for backward compat with V3.0 templates. New producers should emit `modes` and omit `mode`.

figmaName.description: Legacy: Figma display name. The contract top-level key is the canonical name now. Kept for V3.0 round-trip templates.

webflowName.description: Legacy: Webflow display name. The contract top-level key is the canonical name now. Kept for V3.0 round-trip templates.

updatePolicy.description: Legacy: V3.0 update policy values. V3.1+ uses an enum {create, update, skip, delete}. Legacy values (update_value_only, etc.) are still accepted for backward compat.

collection.description: Legacy: Figma collection name (e.g. 'Layout', 'Typography'). Kept for backward compat with V3.0 templates. New producers should derive from namespace instead.

resolvedValue.description: Legacy: pre-computed value with units applied. Kept for V3.0 templates where value + unit were kept separate. New producers should put the resolved value in `value` and use `m.default` for the primary mode.

syncPolicy.description: How this variable should sync between Figma and Webflow.

projectExtension.description: True if this variable is project-specific (not from the Client-First baseline). Required for the baseline contract to allow non-baseline variables.
